stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers, safetensors, and PyTorch.
This indicates continued industry interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest large language models.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: Identifies system scaling, rather than just model scaling, as the next major bottleneck in agentic AI.
Proposes 'scaling the harness,' treating the structured execution layer around foundation models as a primary design focus.
Focuses on designing auditable, persistent, modular, and verifiable architectures for AI agents.

stories.title: DiscoverPhysics: Benchmarking LLMs for Out-of-the-Box Scientific Thinking
stories.author: Carolina Cuesta-Lázaro
bullet_points: Introduces DiscoverPhysics, a new benchmark designed to test genuine scientific reasoning in LLMs.
The benchmark challenges LLMs by requiring them to discover the laws of motion in simulated worlds with non-standard physics (e.g., fractional-power gravity).
It addresses the critical issue of distinguishing between LLM 'reasoning' and mere 'recall' of existing scientific knowledge.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture and utilizes standard ML formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The model is associated with Meta, indicating relevance within the large language model ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The update is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
These models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: The paper argues that the next major bottleneck in agentic AI is 'system scaling,' moving beyond just model scaling.
It introduces the concept of 'scaling the harness,' which focuses on designing robust, auditable, and modular execution architectures around foundation models.
This shift treats the structured execution layer (the 'harness') as a primary object for design and optimization in agentic systems.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep
stories.author: Giulia Fanti
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability issue of Transformer attention mechanisms with long context lengths.
Proposes a 'sleep-like consolidation mechanism' to manage context efficiently.
The method involves periodically converting recent context into persistent fast weights via offline recurrent passes in SSM blocks.

stories.title: Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World
stories.author: Dandan Tu
bullet_points: Introduces 'Claw-Anything,' a new benchmark for evaluating always-on personal assistant LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of current systems operating only on narrow slices of a user's digital world.
Aims to capture performance in broad, context-sensitive, always-on assistance scenarios.

stories.title: Forgetting in Language Models: Capacity, Optimization, and Self-Generated Replay
stories.author: Andrew Gordon Wilson
bullet_points: Addresses catastrophic forgetting in language models, a major challenge in continual learning.
Proposes that self-generated samples from the model's own distribution can serve as effective replay data to mitigate forgetting.
Identifies model capacity as a limiting factor, noting that forgetting persists when capacity is low.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep
stories.author: Giulia Fanti
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability issue of Transformer attention mechanisms with long context lengths.
Proposes a 'sleep-like consolidation mechanism' to manage context memory.
The mechanism involves periodically converting recent context into persistent fast weights via offline recurrent passes in SSM blocks.

stories.title: Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Introduces Auto Benchmark Audit (ABA), an agentic framework designed to verify AI benchmarks.
ABA systematically audits benchmark tasks to find flaws like hidden dependencies and specification gaps.
Addresses the critical issue that current AI benchmarks often contain implicit assumptions and brittle evaluation logic.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) point to its deep learning implementation.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: How clips ate the internet
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: The article discusses the shift in content discovery algorithms across major platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube Shorts.
It highlights the move away from social graph-based feeds (following friends/creators) to algorithmically driven content feeds.
The central theme is how short-form video clips have fundamentally changed the way users consume internet content.

stories.title: Uber president says AI spending is getting ‘harder to justify’
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Uber is reportedly questioning the return on its significant AI spending.
The company has exhausted its annual AI budget early in 2026.
Uber leadership noted a lack of clear connection between increased token consumption (e.g., for Claude Code) and tangible results.

stories.title: A battery-powered Starlink Mini is likely on the way
stories.author: Thomas Ricker
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly developing a battery-powered version of the Starlink Mini dish.
This new iteration aims to provide untethered, portable internet access.
Potential use cases include vanlifers, emergency responders, and remote users.

stories.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The US's significant investment in quantum computing faces potential legal ambiguities.
The initiative includes the launch of the first quantum foundry company.
The article questions the immediate necessity or market need for this new quantum foundry.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was reported as 'mostly successful' in its initial test.
The vehicle is still considered a work in progress and has significant hurdles to overcome.
Full operational capability, including reaching low-Earth orbit, has not yet been achieved.

stories.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
stories.author: Zhaoxiang Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MobileGym, a browser-hosted simulation platform designed for mobile GUI agent research.
Enables verifiable outcomes via deterministic state-based judging using structured JSON states.
Supports scalable online Reinforcement Learning (RL) through low-cost, parallel rollouts without replicating proprietary backends.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of subject-driven image generation, aiming to synthesize new images while preserving a specific subject's identity.
Current methods often struggle with cross-modal reasoning and suffer from artifacts because text and reference images are encoded separately.
The proposed solution conditions diffusion models to better integrate identity preservation with textual instructions, improving upon existing multimodal frameworks.

stories.title: Beyond Summaries: Structure-Aware Labeling of Code Changes with Large Language Models
stories.author: Karen Yorav
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of manual code review becoming difficult due to large-scale software projects and AI code assistants.
It proposes a novel approach using Large Language Models (LLMs) for 'Structure-Aware Labeling' of code changes.
The goal is to automatically classify patch changes (e.g., renames, moves, logic modifications) to improve review efficiency.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs/ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for quantization.
Focuses on ultra-low bit Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace costly MAC operations with bit-shifts.

stories.title: VeriTrace: Evolving Mental Models for Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Alexandra Brintrup
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of information uncertainty and interdependence in deep research agents.
Critiques current methods where intermediate representations rely too heavily on implicit LLM reasoning.
Proposes the need for explicit regulation of an agent's 'mental model' to prevent error propagation.

stories.title: StakeBench: Evaluating Language Understanding Grounded in Market Commitment
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Introduces StakeBench, a new evaluation framework for financial NLP.
Unlike traditional benchmarks, StakeBench grounds language understanding in actual market commitment.
It uses real-world data from resolved markets on platforms like Polymarket to derive supervision from observable market behavior.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of subject-driven image generation, focusing on preserving subject identity while adhering to text prompts.
Existing multimodal approaches often fail at identity preservation, leading to artifacts because text and reference images are encoded separately.
The proposed method conditions diffusion models to overcome the limitations of current frameworks in cross-modal reasoning and identity retention.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to solve engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is crucial for enabling real-world deployment of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) by allowing continuous adaptation to new tasks.
Current MCIT research is limited because existing methods require direct and complex modifications to the base MLLM codebase.

stories.title: Looped Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Dongmin Park
bullet_points: Introduces LoopMDM, a novel architecture for Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) to address underexplored transformer design in this domain.
The core innovation involves selectively looping early-middle transformer layers during training.
This looping technique demonstrably improves both the training efficiency and the overall performance of MDMs.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs and ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for multiplier-free quantization.
Focuses on leveraging logarithmic Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace MAC operations with bit-shifts for efficiency.

stories.title: Active Query Synthesis for Preference Learning
stories.author: Mark A. Davenport
bullet_points: The research addresses the high cost of labeled data in learning user preferences for decision-making systems.
It proposes an improvement to active learning methods to reduce computational expense associated with pool-based evaluation.
A key innovation is accounting for feedback reliability, recognizing that queries between very similar or dissimilar items yield low-confidence responses.

stories.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
stories.author: Zhaoxiang Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MobileGym, a browser-hosted simulation platform designed for mobile GUI agent research.
Provides verifiable outcome signals by using deterministic state-based judging over structured JSON state.
Enables scalable online Reinforcement Learning (RL) through low-cost, parallel rollouts without needing proprietary backends.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to solve engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is crucial for allowing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to continuously adapt to new, real-world tasks.
Current research is limited because existing MCIT methods require direct, complex modifications to the base MLLM codebase.

stories.title: StakeBench: Evaluating Language Understanding Grounded in Market Commitment
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Introduces StakeBench, a new evaluation framework for financial NLP.
Unlike existing benchmarks, StakeBench grounds language understanding in verifiable market commitments (e.g., Polymarket/Manifold data).
Supervision for the framework is derived directly from observable market behavior, linking comments to actual positions and odds.

stories.title: WhoSaidIt: Human-LLM Collaborative Annotation for Text-Based Multilingual Speaker-Attribute Classification
stories.author: Jackie Lee
bullet_points: Addresses the ambiguity of annotating speaker attributes from text, especially in multilingual contexts.
Proposes a novel human-LLM collaborative re-annotation framework to stabilize labels under resource constraints.
Uses iterative interaction with experts and LLMs to surface recurring annotation rationales from noisy data.

stories.title: When Gradients Collide: Failure Modes of Multi-Objective Prompt Optimization for LLM Judges
stories.author: Abhishek Divekar
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of multi-objective prompt optimization for LLM judges, where multiple evaluation criteria must be optimized simultaneously.

Existing gradient methods produce natural-language critiques instead of numerical vectors, preventing the direct application of standard multi-task learning conflict-resolution toolkits (like PCGrad).
The research investigates five different decomposition modes for textual gradient optimizers to resolve these multi-objective conflicts.

stories.title: Peak-Then-Collapse and the Four Interface Channels of Knowledge-Graph Tool Use
stories.author: Dimitar Kazakov
bullet_points: The research investigates the 'peak-then-collapse' phenomenon in Reinforcement Learning (RL) when LLMs use knowledge-graph tools.
Using GRPO on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct with a minimal knowledge-graph API, the tool-grounded answer rate initially improves but subsequently collapses.
The study identifies four recurring failure modes across different reward designs, offering insights into LLM tool-use reliability.

stories.title: CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists
stories.author: Hao Peng
bullet_points: Introduces CausaLab, a new scalable environment designed for evaluating interactive causal discovery.
The environment specifically tests LLM agents on two fronts: solving problems using causal evidence and validating the underlying causal hypothesis.
CausaLab simulates a synthetic laboratory setting where agents interact with measurements and interventions to predict outcomes.

stories.title: Cursor 3 ships parallel AI agents. Here is the multi-agent workflow that actually works.
stories.author: thegdsks
bullet_points: Cursor 3 introduces parallel AI agents into its platform.
The article details a functional multi-agent workflow within the new version.
This suggests an advancement in AI-assisted coding/development tools.

stories.title: Model Poisoning: The Hidden Risk in Supply Chain AI
stories.author: falconsedge68483
bullet_points: The article addresses Model Poisoning, a security risk in AI supply chains.
It highlights a shift in focus from traditional perimeter security (API endpoints, input filtering).
The topic is relevant to AI security but is presented as a discussion rather than a major breakthrough.
